titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Danny Fields Papers
titlestmt.author: Jim Fisher, Leigh Golden, Matthew Gorham, and Michael Rush

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday May 25, 2025 at 23:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 9, 12, 18, 27-30, and 35-42, 85-95 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 10, 43, 54, 59, and 85 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Materials in the June 2019 acquisition are in progress (pending conservation treatment/housing) and are closed until processed.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Booksellers, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013. Additional materials received in August 2015 and June 2019 under the terms of the original purchase agreement.
May 2019 acquisition: Purchased from Louis Jordan on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Danny Fields Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Danny Fields Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: August 2013 acquisition; May 2019 acquisition; June 2019 acquisition.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Danny Fields
bioghist.p: Danny Fields (born Daniel Feinberg, 1939) is an American author, artist manager, and music promoter. In the 1960s he worked with Gloria Stavers as an editor at various teen magazines. In that capacity he befriended photographer Linda Eastman (later McCartney). Fields was part of the downtown New York scene in the 1970s, with connections to both Andy Warhol's Factory circle and the nascent punk scene. He served as a manager for various bands, most notably The Ramones, and worked in varying capacities with the Velvet Underground, the Stooges, the Modern Lovers, MC5, and the Doors among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The archive consists of correspondence, writings, scrapbooks, photographs, business papers, personal papers, interviews, printed material, audiovisual material, and computer media documenting the life and career of Danny Fields, including his work as a promoter and manager in the music business, notably as manager of the Ramones, his association with Andy Warhol's Factory circle, and his work as a writer and editor of books and magazines, including Dream On: Livin' on the Edge with Steven Tyler & Aerosmith (with Cyrinda Foxe-Tyler, Berkley Boulevard Books, 2000), the biography Linda McCartney: A Portrait (Warner, 2001) and magazines including Datebook, Hits , and Rock Scene , among others. The archive also includes papers related to teen magazine editor Gloria Stavers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into seven series: I. Managerial papers, 1970 – 2013; II. Writings and projects, 1960 – 2000; III. Interviews, 1973-2012; IV. Gloria Stavers papers, 1960 – 2008; V. Personal papers, 1952 – 2014; VI. Subject files, 1960 – 2010; VII. Audiovisual materials, circa 1960s-2000s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of a variety of materials documenting Danny Fields' career as a writer, editor, and music industry figure, as well as matterials documenting his personal life.

did.unittitle: Joey Ramone book, drafts, printed emails, clippings and other materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, Ramones fan mail, "Punk" magazine notes, Ramones movie material, notes, manuscripts.

did.unittitle: Tommy Ramone, Monty Melnick, Matt Starr, Seth Abrams, interview transcripts, and email correspondence
Joey and Marky, Howard Stern interview transcripts, Daniel Rey, Arturo Vega interview transcripts
Ramona Musical by Ethan Silverman, drafts and email correspondence
Business papers, correspondence, contracts and telagrams
Joey Ramone book, drafts, printed emails, clippings and other materials
Empty CD cases "We're Outta Here", 2 copies of "Don't Worry About Me",

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of audiovisual materials may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: CDs, "I Wanna Be Your Boyfriend"; "The Bowery Electric"; "What a Wonderful World"; untitled

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes digitized photographs by Danny Fields of The Ramones and New York City hustlers, or "richies".

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: No physical media exists. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of audiovisual material may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Managerial agreements, clippings, correspondence, business papers, and photographs
Concert and tour files, music publishing files, record company files
Biographical files, clippings, concert and tour files, music video production files
Album production files, clippings, concert and tour files, contracts, radio promotion files

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of audiovisual materials may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Paleface, "Trouble in the Country," "Liar," "Burn + Rob," and interview, CMJ, Vista Hotel, NYC, 10/90
did.materialspec: A movie featuring C. Thomas Howell appears at 00:15:25 until the end of the tape.

did.unittitle: Paleface, "Trouble in the Country," rough cut, 1/13/1992
did.materialspec: A time code appears throughout the tape.
Copyright on the film reads 1991.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape has a small note that reads: From: Danny Fields

did.materialspec: This tape is referred to as a master.

did.unittitle: Paleface, 9/9/97, 1.) Cruise Control in Manhattan. 2.) Hard Core
Danny!, final mixes, Big City Cat, Laurel, GCS, Guy?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily documents Danny Fields' career as a writer and editor, particularly his work as a music journalist. It includes materials related to his books, Linda McCartney: A Portrait and Dream On: Livin' on the edge with Steven Tyler and Aerosmith (written with Cyrinda Fox-Tyler); full issues of magazines that Fields edited, including 16 , Country Rhythms , Rock Scene , and Rock Video ; and clippings and drafts of articles and columns written for other periodicals. It also includes materials related to Fields' Rock and Roll Tour of New York, a business he operated in New York City during the 1990s.

title.part: Dream On: Livin' on the edge with Steven Tyler & Aerosmith

did.unittitle: Cyrinda Foxe interview transcripts, Leg McNeil and Gillian McCain
Dream On proof, hardcover, and paperback

Yoko Ono interview, Linda McCartney Remembered
Loves Full Glory: The Story of Linda McCartney, miniseries script for night one
Life Magazine - "The Case of the 'Missing' Beatle: Paul is still with us"
Interview transcripts, printed material, magazines, scripts, miscellaneous correspondence
Fields' research and "personal stuff" for Linda McCartney
Correspondence, clippings, writings, notes, interviews, various printed material, including magazines
Interview material, emails, writings, and correspondence
Writings, correspondence, photographic prints, negatives, slides
Printed materials by Linda McCartney, Wide Open , Linda McCartney's Home Cooking , Linda McCartney on Tour , The World Wildlife Fund: 1991 Annual Report
Sixties: Portrait of an Era, by Linda McCartney
Roadworks: Photographs and Words, by Linda McCartney

did.materialspec: The quality of the tape audio and video is poor, but viewable.
Audio has a little bit of a sync buzz in it.

did.unittitle: "Linda McCartney On Tour: Video News Release"
"Paul and Linda McCartney On the Simpsons"
"Linda McCartney Wide Prairie EPK Featuring Linda and Paul McCartney"

did.materialspec: There are seven segments of black in the video (for commercial insertion for TV). Six of the segments are 00:04:45 long and one is 30 seconds long.
NTSC. MONO.

did.unittitle: "TV Tributes to Linda, The Animals' Angel"
did.materialspec: NTSC
NTSC
Note with the tape reads: Some good audio. Watch it first. Leave out other people. NTSC.
NTSC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There's a note attached to the tape that reads: Dear D - I watched most of this. Left it cued up to your hug with Stella.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The VH1 Rock 'n' Roll Hall of Fame: Class of '99 Inductees for 1999 include Billy Joel, Paul McCartney, Bruce Springsteen and Dusty Springfield.

did.materialspec: NTSC

did.unittitle: Audiofiles related to Linda McCartney, 3 DVD-R

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs, contact sheets, photographic negatives, writings, and correspondence related to the pop group, the Hudson Brothers, who were covered heavily in 16 Magazine during Fields' tenure as editor of the publication.

did.unittitle: Rock and Roll Tour of New York
"Rock book proposal", "From the suede carrying case", Rock and Roll Tour NYC, 1994
"Incentives", "New music seminar", "t-shirt design", Rock and Roll Tour NYC
"Things to do", Bio, "Brochure", Rock and Roll Tour NYC
"Charities", "tour license", "share holders", Rock and Roll Tour NYC
Press including layouts for publicists map
"I Tripped" Rock and Roll Tour t-shirt

did.unittitle: "Important CD's - interviews, B'casts, orig casts, etc.", 37 compact discs
Digitized interviews from cassette albums, 1 TB external hard drive
Loose pages of 16 Magazine and Spec
Celebrity photos from 16 Magazine used in Who's Your Fave Rave
Correspondence, clippings, and other papers related to the publication of Who's Your Fave Rave?
Proof for Who's Your Fave Rave?
Materials related to Al Pacino, Scorpio, and The Godfather Part II
Press photographs, Bay City Rollers tour schedule
"The Sales History of 16 Magazine and What it Tells Us"
Contact sheet and negatives of pictures of Richard Young
Stavers clippings related to The Beatles and others
Copies of handwritten lyrics by Jim Morrison
Clippings from 16 Magazine compiled for Who's Your Fave Rave?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Screenplays for movies and television shows including Kojack, The Great Houdinis, Ripper and Dillinger, and Sweeter in the Morning and press kits for television shows The Original American Bandstand Dancers, Charles in Charge, and Shining Time Station.

did.unittitle: Celebrity Magazine mockups, clippings, research, and other papers
Bay City Rollers fold-out insert mockup

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, professional correspondence, photocopies of photographs, and other papers.

did.unittitle: Gloria Stavers will and legal documents
Gloria Stavers death and funeral service
Jim Morrison and Beatles clippings, notes and correspondence relating to use of photographs
Press kit for The History of British Rock
Bay City Rollers photographs and band information
Stavers correspondence regarding a book proposal
Stavers annotated clippings relating to rock 'n' roll and Jim Morrison
Papers relating to The Doors and photographs of Jim Morrison

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes buttons and a keychain for Jim Morrison and The Doors, pins and a button for The Beatles, and other buttons and pins.

did.unittitle: Stavers papers and legal correspondence relating to photograph licensing and copyright
Spec Magazine and 16 Magazine, bound edition
Gloria Stavers Story [Who's Your Fave Rave?] book proposal
Contact sheet of photographs of Bob Dylan
Beatles contact sheets, Help! press conference and other locations, and negative
Photographs of Gloria Stavers with celebrities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most photographs are promotional stills.

Enlarged Beatles contact sheets, Help! press conference, outdoors

did.unittitle: AUDIO GLORIA STAVERS + DANNY FIELDS DISCUSS DAVID CASSIDY 1 HOUR

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series comprises personal papers of Danny Fields and includes education records from high school through graduate school; biographical materials; materials related to an unpublished memoir by Fields; and materials related to Fields' travels. It also includes materials related to a lawsuit carried out by Fields against Out magazine, as well as materials related to the murder of Linda Stein, Fields' partner as co-manager of the Ramones.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typewritten drafts and correspondence related to an unpublished memoir by Danny Fields

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series comprises files maintained by Danny Fields related to his professional and personal interests, including people involved in music and the arts, the downtown New York City arts scene, 1960s-1970s counterculture, and movies and theatre. Materials in the files inclcude clippings, photographs, correspondence, and ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials in this folder are related to a rock band from the 1960s called The Knack, not the 1980s band of the same name.

did.unittitle: Big Sur Folk Festival and Celebration of Life festival, McCrea Louisiana
Downtown New York City arts scene

title.part: The Official Punk Rock Book of Lists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains a newspaper clipping concerning a 1970s rock band called the Velvet Underground, not the more well-known American band of the same name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials related to many people who were part of Andy Warhol's social and professional circle, including Taylor Mead, Jackie Curtis, Gerard Malanga, Candy Darling, Amos Poe, Ronald Tavel, and Ultraviolet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of 25 video guides in which Danny Fields describes the materials compiled in the scrapbooks that are included in this collection.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material (computer media). Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of 25 scrapbooks compiled by Danny Fields.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: In-process material [pending conservation treatment/housing.] For further information, consult Access Services.
